spec: SCALEBENCH v1 + WS amendment; results-v3 campaign

env_sets.pinned_giloff: JSC_useJSThreads=1 JSC_useThreadGIL=0 JSC_useVMLite=1 JSC_useSharedAtomStringTable=1 JSC_useSharedGCHeap=1 JSC_useThreadGILOffUnsafe=1

handicap: GC under JS threads on this branch is stop-the-world with parallel marking; concurrent marking (JSC_useConcurrentSharedGCMarking=1) was A/B-tested here and crashes intermittently at teardown. Go and Java ship fully concurrent collectors.
protocol_notes: 3 measured reps per cell (5 for the W=1 flag-tax cells), median reported; NO discarded warmup rep (task override of SPEC 5-rep+warmup protocol).
Phase-5 naive-arm comparators at W=8/32/64 are REUSED from the main ladders (same binary, same env, same session), not re-run; WS cells were run fresh.

Plain jsc (zero flags) cannot run bench.js: ReferenceError: Lock is not defined (Thread API requires JSC_useJSThreads=1); recorded as N/A with one demonstration rep (exit 3). Closest baseline measured instead: JSC_useJSThreads=1 only (GIL on).

/tmp (tmpfs) filled with core dumps at ~21:53 UTC during the first js-congc W=2 cell (two 6-19GB cores from the SIGSEGVs); cores deleted, core dumps disabled (RLIMIT_CORE=0) for all subsequent reps, the affected cell fully re-run. RAM was never under pressure (247GB total).
js-congc W=2 has 6 recorded reps (3 pre-cleanup incl. 2 SIGSEGVs, 3 post-cleanup all green); both batches kept in raw data.
Host quiet throughout: no jsc/ninja/java/go processes at start; 1-min loadavg 1.03-1.94 at batch starts.
findings: CRASH (concurrent marking): intermittent SIGSEGV with JSC_useConcurrentSharedGCMarking=1. Signature (from core, W=2): GCSegmentedArray<JSCell const*>::removeLast <- HeapClientSet::flushClientMutatorMarkStackForExit (Source/JavaScriptCore/heap/Heap.cpp:6953) <- GCClient::Heap::detachCurrentThread (Heap.cpp:7131) <- tearDownSpawnedThreadForExit (ThreadManager.cpp:687) <- threadMain. Spawned-thread teardown races the concurrent shared-GC mark stack. Hit rates: W=2 2/6, W=4 3/3, W=8 2/3, W=16 0/3, W=32 0/3, W=48 0/3, W=64 1/3. STW arm: 0 crashes in 24 runs.

GC-mode A/B: where congc completes, totals are within noise of STW (W=1 33.7s vs 33.3s; W=16 48.2 vs 47.6; W=32 75.7 vs 76.6; W=48 78.7 vs 78.7; W=64 83.6 vs 81.3). Concurrent shared GC marking buys ~nothing on this workload in its current state and is not stable.

JS NEGATIVE SCALING: js-stw W=1 33.26s -> W=2 45.49s (0.73x) -> W=4 49.96s (0.67x) -> W=8 48.12s -> W=16 47.56s -> W=32 76.56s (0.43x) -> W=48 78.67s -> W=64 81.32s (0.41x). cpu_util falls from 1.18 (W=1) to 0.09 (W=64): the program is almost fully serialized and adding threads adds pure overhead, with a second cliff at W=32 driven by Phase A (51.4s vs 27.6s at W=8).

Go scales: 1.74s -> 0.36s (4.9x at W=48, knee at W=16-32). Java scales to W=8-16 (1.85s -> 0.93s, 2.0x) then degrades slightly. Cross-language gap at W=1: JS pinned 33.3s vs Go 1.74s (19x) vs Java 1.85s (18x); at W=64: 81.3s vs 0.36s (228x) / 1.14s (71x).

WS-vs-naive Phase C: JS C-phase 555.8 vs 632.4 ms (W=8), 469.3 vs 651.3 (W=32), 391.6 vs 597.4 (W=64) - WS helps Phase C 12-34% but is invisible in totals (C is <1% of total). Go C: 2.1 vs 10.7 (W=8), 2.1 vs 11.0 (W=64). Java C: 42.0 vs 57.9 (W=8) but 213.3 vs 159.5 (W=32, WS SLOWER), 247.0 vs 180.3 (W=64, WS slower). All WS checksums identical to naive (parity holds).

FLAG TAX (W=1, 5 reps): GIL-on minimal (useJSThreads=1) median 15.14s / 433MB RSS / cpu 1.03; pinned GIL-off median 33.69s / 14.27GB RSS / cpu 1.18. The GIL-off flag set costs +123% wall and 33x RSS on single-threaded execution. Zero-flag jsc: N/A (Lock is not defined).

RSS: JS pinned runs hold 11.8-14.3GB across ALL W (vs Go ~0.2GB, Java ~0.9GB) - the shared GC heap under VMLite appears to retain order-of-magnitude more memory than the GIL-on configuration (0.43GB).
Regression vs run1 (2026-06-10 binary): js W=1 total 24.31s then, 33.26s now (+37%); but run1 had ALL js W>1 cells failing - today's binary completes the full ladder.
